findings.message: record_raw_path stores the raw full path (including query string) as the metric key with no sanitize, no distinct-value cap, and no "other" overflow bucket. Every distinct URL+query becomes a new series, producing unbounded label cardinality that will blow up Prometheus. This is inconsistent with every sibling signal in this class, which is bounded via _san + _bump_capped (MAX_ROUTES/MAX_ATTRIBUTION/MAX_REFERRERS).
findings.suggestion: Strip the query string, sanitize via _san(), and bump through _bump_capped(self._raw_paths, route, MAX_ROUTES, overflow="other") so the label is a sanitized, capped enum with an overflow bucket. Better still, drop this signal entirely since app_frontend_page_views_total already records views by bounded route.

findings.message: app_frontend_raw_path_total emits one series per raw full path with the value rendered directly into the path="..." label (no sanitize, no cap). Unbounded high-cardinality label: a single crawler or campaign with varying query strings yields unlimited series and unbounded /metrics growth.
findings.suggestion: Render from a sanitized, capped store (see record_raw_path fix) so the path label is a fixed-size set with an "other" overflow series, or remove this counter in favor of the existing bounded app_frontend_page_views_total.
